api/client/python/examples.purpose: Runnable Python example scripts (sync and async) that demonstrate openmeter SDK usage patterns against a live endpoint; each script is self-contained and exercises one domain area. Serves as both documentation and a Pyright-checked regression surface for the generated SDK.

patterns.name: Environment-variable configuration
patterns.description: Endpoint and token are always read from OPENMETER_ENDPOINT and OPENMETER_TOKEN env vars with sensible defaults; never hardcoded.

patterns.name: HttpResponseError catch-all
patterns.description: All SDK calls are wrapped in try/except corehttp.exceptions.HttpResponseError to surface API errors consistently.

patterns.name: Dict-access for discriminated-union items
patterns.description: SDK responses for polymorphic list items (e.g. entitlements) are dicts; use .get() not attribute access.

patterns.name: One-file-per-domain organisation
patterns.description: Each domain (ingest, query, entitlement, subscription) lives in its own file under sync/ or async/; no cross-domain imports between example files.

patterns.name: Async examples use openmeter.aio.Client exclusively
patterns.description: Files under async/ import from openmeter.aio and use async with + asyncio.run(); sync/ files import from openmeter and call main() directly.

key_file_guides.role: Poetry project referencing the parent SDK via path dependency (develop=true); pin python ^3.9 to match SDK floor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add a version constraint on openmeter here — the path dependency must track the local checkout.

key_file_guides.role: Pyright type-checking config covering both async/ and sync/ directories at basic mode.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mypy is explicitly not supported due to overloaded constructors; never add mypy config.

key_file_guides.role: Reference implementation for async CloudEvents ingest; shows async with client, event construction, and error handling.
key_file_guides.watch_for: asyncio.run() must wrap the top-level coroutine; calling it inside an already-running loop raises RuntimeError.

key_file_guides.role: Structural mirror of async/ingest.py using blocking Client; module-level client construction followed by direct main() call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must not wrap code in async with or import from openmeter.aio.

anti_patterns: Importing from openmeter.aio in sync/ examples or from openmeter (sync) in async/ examples
Hardcoding endpoint or token instead of reading OPENMETER_ENDPOINT / OPENMETER_TOKEN
Using attribute access on dict-typed discriminated-union SDK responses instead of .get()
Adding cross-domain logic or shared helpers across example files — each must remain self-contained
Running pyright type checks with mypy — mypy is incompatible with the generated SDK's overloaded constructors

decisions.decision: Async and sync examples are structurally identical folders with separate client imports
decisions.rationale: Keeps both flavours discoverable and ensures every domain is covered in both execution models without a shared abstraction that could obscure the per-client differences.
The generated SDK uses overloaded constructors that mypy cannot handle; Pyright is the only supported static type checker.

code_examples.code:
import asyncio, os
from azure.core.exceptions import HttpResponseError
from openmeter.aio import Client

client = Client(
    endpoint=os.environ.get('OPENMETER_ENDPOINT', 'https://openmeter.cloud'),
    credential=os.environ.get('OPENMETER_TOKEN', ''),
)

async def main():
    async with client:
        try:
            await client.ingest_events(...)
        except HttpResponseError as e:
            print(e)

asyncio.run(main())
